title: A dataset recorded during development of an affective brain-computer music interface: calibration session
description: This dataset contains electroencephalogram (EEG), galvanic skin response (GSR), and electrocardiogram (ECG) recordings from 19 healthy adult participants during a calibration session of an affective brain-computer music interface system. Participants listened to 40-second synthetic music clips designed to induce specific affective states defined by valence and arousal dimensions across 5 runs of 18 trials each. The dataset includes self-reported affective state ratings and auxiliary variables, serving as calibration data for real-time online modification of music to induce target emotional states. This is the first dataset in a 3-dataset series (calibration, offline training, online real-time control). This dataset accompanies the publication by Daly et al. (2018) in Scientific Data.
methods_description: EEG, GSR, and ECG data were recorded at 1 kHz sampling rate from 19 healthy adult participants during listening to synthetic music clips (40 s duration). Each clip targeted two affective states sequentially (first 20 s for state 1, remaining 20 s for state 2), with 5 runs of 18 music trials per session. Participants provided self-reported valence and arousal ratings for each trial. The synthetic music clips were generated using a real-time music generator based on target emotional states defined by valence (LOW/NEUTRAL/HIGH) and arousal (LOW/NEUTRAL/HIGH) dimensions.
